---
title: "RNAseq_sPLSDA_tool"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
##############################################################################
##  sPLS-DA pipeline for RNA-seq counts – header-encoded sample metadata
##  Requires only ONE CSV file with #NAME / #CLASS rows
##############################################################################

####  USER SETTINGS  #########################################################
count_file     <- "ExpAnalyst_format.csv"  # see format above
min_count      <- 10                # gene filter
top_var_genes  <- 5000              # keep most-variable genes
ncomp_try      <- 2:4               # components to test
keepX_grid     <- c(50, 100, 200)   # genes to test per component
cv_folds       <- 5                 # M-fold CV
cv_repeats     <- 3                 # repeat CV this many times
out_dir        <- "splsda_results"
##############################################################################

dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

####  1.  PACKAGES  ###########################################################
pkgs <- c("DESeq2", "mixOmics", "ggplot2", "readr", "dplyr")
for (p in pkgs)
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages(p, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
library(DESeq2); library(mixOmics); library(ggplot2); library(dplyr)

####  2.  READ COUNTS + METADATA FROM HEADER  #################################
raw <- readr::read_csv(count_file, col_names = FALSE,
                       progress = FALSE, show_col_types = FALSE)

# --- extract header rows ----------------------------------------------------
sample_ids <- as.character(unlist(raw[1, -1]))
group_vec  <- as.character(unlist(raw[2, -1]))

# --- build count matrix -----------------------------------------------------
counts <- raw[-c(1, 2), ]                  # drop header rows
gene_ids <- counts[[1]]                    # first column = gene IDs
counts   <- as.matrix(counts[, -1])        # numeric part
mode(counts) <- "numeric"

rownames(counts) <- gene_ids
colnames(counts) <- sample_ids

# basic sanity
stopifnot(length(sample_ids) == ncol(counts),
          length(group_vec)  == ncol(counts))

meta <- data.frame(sample = sample_ids,
                   group  = factor(group_vec))

####  3.  GENE PRE-FILTERING  #################################################
keep <- rowSums(counts) >= min_count
counts <- counts[keep, , drop = FALSE]

####  4.  VARIANCE-STABILISING TRANSFORM  ####################################
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                              colData   = meta,
                              design    = ~ 1)           # no DE design
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)
vst_mat <- assay(vsd)

# keep top-variable genes
vars <- apply(vst_mat, 1, var)
top  <- names(sort(vars, decreasing = TRUE))[seq_len(min(top_var_genes,
                                                         nrow(vst_mat)))]
vst_mat <- vst_mat[top, ]

####  5.  PREP FOR mixOmics  ##################################################
X <- t(vst_mat)                 # samples × genes
Y <- meta$group                 # factor

####  6–7.  TUNING  +  FINAL MODEL  ###########################################
set.seed(123)

## ---- 6a.  pick sensible CV folds -------------------------------------------
min_cl <- min(table(Y))          # smallest class size
if (cv_folds > min_cl) {
  warning("Reducing cv_folds from ", cv_folds,
          " to ", min_cl, " (min class size).")
  cv_folds <- min_cl
}

## ---- 6b.  run tuner (ask for at most 'max_comp' components) -----------------
max_comp <- max(ncomp_try)            # e.g. 4
tune <- tune.splsda(
  X, Y,
  ncomp        = max_comp,
  test.keepX   = keepX_grid,
  dist         = "max.dist",
  validation   = "Mfold",
  folds        = cv_folds,
  nrepeat      = cv_repeats,
  progressBar  = FALSE
)

## ---- 6c.  harvest tuning choices -------------------------------------------
best_ncomp <- as.integer(unlist(tune$choice.ncomp))[1]   # works for list or scalar

if (is.list(tune$choice.keepX)) {
  ## Newer mixOmics: list of numeric vectors (one per component)
  best_keepX <- unlist(tune$choice.keepX)
} else {
  ## Older mixOmics: already a numeric vector
  best_keepX <- tune$choice.keepX
}

## ---- 6d.  safety cap on component count ------------------------------------
n_allowed <- nrow(X) - 1                                     # ≤ samples − 1
if (best_ncomp > n_allowed) {
  warning("Reducing ncomp from ", best_ncomp,
          " to ", n_allowed, " (limited by sample size).")
  best_ncomp <- n_allowed
  best_keepX <- best_keepX[seq_len(best_ncomp)]
}

## ---- 7.  FINAL MODEL --------------------------------------------------------
model <- splsda(
  X, Y,
  ncomp = best_ncomp,
  keepX = best_keepX
)

saveRDS(model, file = file.path(out_dir, "splsda_model.rds"))

####  9.  PLOTS  ##############################################################
pdf(file.path(out_dir, "sample_scores.pdf"), 7, 6)
plotIndiv(model, comp = 1:2, group = Y,
          legend = TRUE, ellipse = TRUE, ind.names = FALSE)
dev.off()

pdf(file.path(out_dir, "loadings_comp1.pdf"), 7, 6)
plotLoadings(model, comp = 1, contrib = "max", method = "mean")
dev.off()

#### 10. EXPORT SELECTED GENES  ###############################################
library(dplyr)            # bind_rows()

sel_df <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(best_ncomp), function(k) {
  v <- selectVar(model, comp = k)          # $name and $value vectors
  data.frame(
    Component = k,
    Gene      = v$name,
    Loading   = v$value,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

readr::write_csv(sel_df,
                 file.path(out_dir, "selected_genes_by_component.csv"))

message("✓ Selected-gene table written to ",
        file.path(out_dir, "selected_genes_by_component.csv"))




``` 


```{r}
###############################################################################
##  ORA / GSEA of sPLS-DA selected genes
###############################################################################
library(clusterProfiler)
library(ReactomePA)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(org.Gg.eg.db)      # ← change if not chicken
library(KEGGREST)
library(dplyr)
library(openxlsx)

# ---------- 1. Read selected genes -------------------------------------------
sel_df <- readr::read_csv(file.path(out_dir, 
                                    "selected_genes_by_component.csv"))

# ---------- 2. Map to ENTREZ --------------------------------------------------
# Detect whether your gene column is SYMBOL or ENSEMBL
is_ens <- all(grepl("^ENS", sel_df$Gene))
keytype <- if (is_ens) "ENSEMBL" else "SYMBOL"

map_cols <- if (keytype == "ENSEMBL") c("ENTREZID", "SYMBOL") else c("ENTREZID")
gene_map <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Gg.eg.db,
  keys     = sel_df$Gene,
  keytype  = keytype,
  columns  = map_cols
)

sel_df  <- sel_df |> left_join(gene_map, by = setNames(keytype, "Gene"))
sel_df  <- sel_df |> filter(!is.na(ENTREZID))

# ---------- 3. Define universe -----------------------------------------------
gene_universe <- rownames(vst_mat)               # all expressed genes
univ_map <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Gg.eg.db,
  keys     = gene_universe,
  keytype  = keytype,
  columns  = "ENTREZID"
)
gene_universe_entrez <- unique(na.omit(univ_map$ENTREZID))

sig_genes <- unique(sel_df$ENTREZID)             # test set

# ---------- 4a. GO-BP ORA -----------------------------------------------------
ego <- enrichGO(
  gene          = sig_genes,
  universe      = gene_universe_entrez,
  OrgDb         = org.Gg.eg.db,
  keyType       = "ENTREZID",
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  readable      = TRUE
)

# ---------- 4b. KEGG ORA ------------------------------------------------------
kegg_code <- "gga"                               # change for other species
kmap <- keggLink("pathway", kegg_code)
kegg_term2gene <- data.frame(
  term = sub("path:",  "", unname(kmap)),
  gene = sub(paste0(kegg_code, ":"), "", names(kmap)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
kdesc <- keggList("pathway", kegg_code)
kegg_term2name <- data.frame(
  term = sub("path:", "", names(kdesc)),
  name = sub(" - .*", "", unname(kdesc)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
ekegg <- enricher(
  gene          = sig_genes,
  universe      = gene_universe_entrez,
  TERM2GENE     = kegg_term2gene,
  TERM2NAME     = kegg_term2name,
  pAdjustMethod = "BH"
) |> setReadable(org.Gg.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID")

# ---------- 4c. Reactome ORA --------------------------------------------------
ereact <- enrichPathway(
  gene          = sig_genes,
  universe      = gene_universe_entrez,
  organism      = "chicken",         # human = "human", mouse = "mouse"
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  readable      = TRUE
)

## ---------- 5. Preranked GSEA (GO-BP) ---------------------------------------

library(dplyr)

# ---- 5a.  pick the loading-weight column automatically ----------------------
weight_col <- setdiff(
  names(sel_df),
  c("Component", "Gene", "ENTREZID", "SYMBOL", "GENEID")
) |>
  (\(x) x[vapply(sel_df[x], is.numeric, logical(1))])() |>
  {\(.) .[1]}()                              # first numeric among the remainder

if (is.na(weight_col))
  stop("No numeric loading column found in sel_df")

message("Using '", weight_col, "' as the ranking weight.")

# ---- 5b.  one gene → one weight (largest |loading|) -------------------------
rank_vec <- sel_df %>%
  group_by(ENTREZID) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(.data[[weight_col]]), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(desc(.data[[weight_col]])) %>%
  transmute(ENTREZID, rank = .data[[weight_col]]) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

# ---- 5c.  preranked GSEA (GO-BP) -------------------------------------------
gsea_go <- gseGO(
  geneList      = rank_vec,
  OrgDb         = org.Gg.eg.db,      # change org if not chicken
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH"
)

# ---------- 6. Save results ---------------------------------------------------
as_df <- function(res) {
  if (is.null(res) || nrow(as.data.frame(res)) == 0)
    return(data.frame())
  as.data.frame(res)
}

write.xlsx(list(
  GO_ORA   = as_df(ego),
  KEGG_ORA = as_df(ekegg),
  Reactome = as_df(ereact),
  GO_GSEA  = as_df(gsea_go, "core_enrichment")
), file = file.path(out_dir, "splsda_enrichment_results.xlsx"),
   overwrite = TRUE)

message("✓ ORA/GSEA results written to ",
        file.path(out_dir, "splsda_enrichment_results.xlsx"))



```
